# host_mac_audio_multi_output (Resource)

Manages one macOS CoreAudio multi-output device.

Use this resource to create or adopt an Audio MIDI Setup multi-output device without managing CoreAudio plist files directly.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_brew" "blackhole_2ch" {
  name         = "blackhole-2ch"
  package_type = "cask"
}

data "host_mac_audio_device" "blackhole_2ch" {
  name = "BlackHole 2ch"

  depends_on = [
    host_package_brew.blackhole_2ch,
  ]
}

resource "host_mac_audio_multi_output" "default" {
  name = "Multi-Output Device"

  primary_device = {
    uid = data.host_mac_audio_device.blackhole_2ch.uid
  }

  devices = [
    {
      builtin_output = "headphones"
    },
    {
      uid = data.host_mac_audio_device.blackhole_2ch.uid
    },
  ]

  sample_rate_hz = 48000
  default_output = false
  system_output  = false

  depends_on = [
    host_package_brew.blackhole_2ch,
  ]
}
```

## Device Selectors

Each device selector must set exactly one of `uid`, `name`, or `builtin_output`.

Use `name` or `uid` for external and virtual audio devices. Supported built-in output selectors are `headphones` and `speakers`.

## Existing Devices

When `uid` is omitted, the provider adopts an existing multi-output device with the same `name` if exactly one exists. Otherwise it creates a device with a provider-owned UID.

## Import

Import an existing multi-output device by CoreAudio UID:

```shell
terraform import host_mac_audio_multi_output.default '~:AMS2_StackedOutput:0'
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `devices` (Attributes List) Ordered output devices included in the multi-output device. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--devices))
- `name` (String) Display name for the multi-output device.
- `primary_device` (Attributes) Primary CoreAudio device used as the multi-output clock source. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--primary_device))

### Optional

- `default_output` (Boolean) Set this multi-output device as the current default output device.
- `delete_on_destroy` (Boolean) Destroy the CoreAudio multi-output device on Terraform destroy. Defaults to false.
- `sample_rate_hz` (Number) Nominal sample rate for the multi-output device. Defaults to 48000.
- `system_output` (Boolean) Set this multi-output device as the current system output device.
- `uid` (String) CoreAudio UID for the multi-output device. Omit to adopt an existing device with the same name or generate a provider-owned UID.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) CoreAudio UID for the managed multi-output device.

<a id="nestedatt--devices"></a>
### Nested Schema for `devices`

Optional:

- `builtin_output` (String) Built-in output selector. Supported values are `headphones` and `speakers`.
- `drift_correction` (Boolean) Enable CoreAudio drift correction for this subdevice.
- `name` (String) CoreAudio device display name. The provider requires exactly one matching device.
- `uid` (String) CoreAudio device UID.

Read-Only:

- `resolved_name` (String) Resolved CoreAudio device display name.
- `resolved_uid` (String) Resolved CoreAudio device UID.


<a id="nestedatt--primary_device"></a>
### Nested Schema for `primary_device`

Optional:

- `builtin_output` (String) Built-in output selector. Supported values are `headphones` and `speakers`.
- `name` (String) CoreAudio device display name. The provider requires exactly one matching device.
- `uid` (String) CoreAudio device UID.

Read-Only:

- `resolved_name` (String) Resolved CoreAudio device display name.
- `resolved_uid` (String) Resolved CoreAudio device UID.
